item.title: Can Frontier Models Autocomplete Safety Research?
item.description: pspanWe pose the following research question: /spanbspanhow can we measure the "research taste" of language models in experiment planning? What parts of planning taste remain intrinsic to humans?/span/bspan /span/ppbr //ppbspanTL;DR./span/bspan A future where “tasteless autoresearch” improves capabi

item.title: Alzheimer's tau protein has a surprising secret role in memory
item.description: Researchers found that tau is essential for turning new experiences into lasting memories by helping organize the brain's memory-storing cells. The mouse study also revealed how abnormal tau may contribute to Alzheimer's by disrupting both the formation of new memories and the recall of existing one

item.title: Physicists recreate black hole energy extraction in the lab
item.description: Researchers have recreated the physics of extracting energy from a spinning black hole using a stationary device that produces synthetic ultrafast rotation. The achievement transforms a long-standing theoretical idea into a practical experiment and could inspire new advances in optics, wireless comm

item.title: The current bottleneck is political will, not research
item.description: pbspanAbstract:/span/b/polli value="1"bspanWe already know enough to act./span/bspan I wish we were in a world where research was the bottleneck, but the main constraint on AI safety is no longer a shortage of clever policy ideas: best practices already exist and are not being applied or enforced, a

item.title: Heavy marijuana smoking may increase cancer risk, researchers warn
item.description: Heavy marijuana smoking may raise the risk of lung cancer and several head and neck cancers, according to growing research, but many important questions remain unanswered. Scientists are still trying to determine how much marijuana use is enough to significantly increase cancer risk. Edibles have no
